# Welcome {.unnumbered}

This is the online version of **Biostatistics Practicum** by
The rgtlab Curriculum Project, a graduate textbook in applied
biostatistics practice.

The book covers the practical workflow that surrounds
statistical modelling: reproducibility, Git infrastructure,
Docker, Quarto reporting, tidyverse data wrangling, and
clinical-trial case studies. Jenny Bryan's STAT 545
[@bryan2019stat545] at UBC is the pedagogical model for this
approach. The companion volume,
[*Statistical Computing in the Age of AI*](https://rgtlab.org/scai),
covers the statistical theory and inference that this book
deliberately excludes.

See the [Preface](preface.qmd) for motivation and the
[Conventions](conventions.qmd) page for visual cues used
throughout.

## License

This book is licensed to you under a
[Creative Commons
Attribution-NonCommercial-NoDerivatives 4.0 International
License](https://creativecommons.org/licenses/by-nc-nd/4.0/).

The code samples in this book are licensed under
[Creative Commons CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0
1.0)](https://creativecommons.org/publicdomain/zero/1.0/),
i.e. public domain.
